LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JEFF PARR, SARA PARR, KEVIN FITZGERALD, JULIA FITZGERALD'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORITONS OF THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED AS TO THE ALTER EGO, AGENCY AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS AND GRANTED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE ATTORNEY'S FEES ALLEGATIONS. THE ALTER EGO, AGENCY AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS ARE SUFFICIENTLY PLED. PLAINTIFFS ACKNOWLEDGE THAT THEIR ATTORNEY'S FEES ALLEGATIONS ARE DEFICIENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Krista M. Ostoich, Attorney at Law, Tel. 831.250.7422, for plaintiffs;
Rebecca L. Epstein, of Seubert French Frimel & Warner LLP, Tel. 650.283.6328, for defendants;
J. Craig Crawford, of Carr, McClellan, Ingersoll, Thompson & Horn, Tel. 650.696.2580, for defendants, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JEFF PARR, SARA PARR, KEVIN FITZGERALD, JULIA FITZGERALD MOTION TO STRIKE poritons of the 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied as to the alter ego, agency and punitive damages allegations and granted with twenty days leave to amend as to the attorney's fees allegations. The alter ego, agency and punitive damages allegations are sufficiently pled. Plaintiffs acknowledge that their attorney's fees allegations are deficient. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Krista M. Ostoich, Attorney at Law, Tel. 831.250.7422, for plaintiffs;
Rebecca L. Epstein, of Seubert French Frimel & Warner LLP, Tel. 650.283.6328, for defendants;
J. Craig Crawford, of Carr, McClellan, Ingersoll, Thompson & Horn, Tel. 650.696.2580, for defendants, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KEVIN FITZGERALD Jeff Parr, Sara Parr, and Julia Fitzgerald's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is: a) overruled as to the first, eighth, tenth, and eleventh causes of action; b) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action; and c) sustained without leave to amend as to the second, third, and ninth causes of action. Plaintiffs adequately allege their first cause of action for breach of contract as to all individual defendants. The first amended complaint adequately alleges the terms of the contract, the defendants' breach, plaintiffs' performance, and damages. The first amended complaint also adequately alleges that all four individual defendants entered into the contract. The eighth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, the tenth cause of action for declaratory relief and the eleventh cause of action for slander are adequately alleged. The breach of fiduciary duty claim is cognizable as a direct claim because it alleges harm to the plaintiffs themselves separate from harm to the company. As long as there are sufficient allegations to support a claim, a demurrer must be overruled, even if the claim contains extraneous allegations such as the eighth cause of action does with regard to the alleged mismanagement of the company. Plaintiffs are not required to plead the exact language of the allegedly slanderous statements or provide the names of individuals who heard the statements. The fourth cause of action for fraud is not pled with sufficient specificity. The first amended complaint does not state when or how the alleged misrepresentations were made. Plaintiffs do not make any substantive opposition to the demurrer to the second, third and ninth causes of action and their failure to do is treated as a concession that the demurrer to those claims is meritorious. (1) Order signed for all Demurrers. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
